meta.name: Asahi repo design DNA
meta.description: Repo-wide page design baseline for Asahi. Extracted from the canonical Next.js homepage and diff viewer, then used as the visual standard for every page in this repo.

color.palette_type: monochromatic with semantic red/green diff accents

primary.role: primary foreground, icons, headings, active controls

accent.role: additions, positive diff markers, green status emphasis

neutral.usage: white content surfaces, light gray chrome, muted gray copy, near-black text, optional dark-mode shell

color.contrast_strategy: dark-on-light dominant with subtle 1px borders; semantic diff colors are pale fills plus saturated side rules/text

typography.font_style_notes: neutral modern sans for UI; compact monospaced code with slight negative tracking for diff readability

spacing.content_density: comfortable on entry pages, compact/data-dense in work surfaces
spacing.section_rhythm: quiet entry stacks use 16px clusters and 32px separators; workspaces use tight 6-12px chrome spacing and full-height panes

layout.grid_system: single-column centered entry pages; desktop workspaces use 320px sidebar plus flexible main surface
layout.max_content_width: 672px for focused entry content; full viewport for reviewer/workspace pages
layout.columns: 1 on entry pages, 2 on desktop workspaces, 1 overlay-style sidebar/main on mobile
layout.gutter: 8px toolbar gaps, 12px sidebar offsets, 24px entry horizontal padding
layout.breakpoints: mobile-first with md around 768px for desktop chrome/grid
layout.alignment_tendency: strict grid in workspaces, centered narrow composition on entry pages

shape.border_usage: subtle 1px neutral borders on inputs, headers, sidebars, and content separators
shape.divider_style: thin neutral hairlines plus semantic red/green diff markers where applicable

elevation.shadow_style: mostly none; shadow reserved for dropdowns and mobile overlays
elevation.depth_cues: surface contrast, borders, sticky headers, slight overlay blur on mobile

motion.entrance_pattern: subtle fade/zoom/slide for menus and mobile sidebars

components.button_style: ghost icon buttons or direct text buttons, 32-36px square/compact, 8px radius, muted foreground with hover background/text shift
components.input_style: transparent text input inside rounded 1px bordered shell; 48px entry height and 36px compact workspace height
components.card_style: avoid marketing cards; use plain work panes and list rows with subtle borders
components.navigation_pattern: top toolbar for primary actions; persistent sidebar or summary rail for workspace context
components.modal_style: rounded bordered popover/dropdown, white or themed surface, shadow-lg; mobile sidebar uses bottom sheet behavior
components.list_style: dense rows with muted metadata, truncation, semantic status marks, and scroll containment
components.component_notes: primary experiences should feel like a fast code-review tool, not a landing page or dashboard kit

aesthetic.visual_metaphor: the code diff and review workflow are the brand language
aesthetic.era_influence: modern GitHub-adjacent developer tooling with shadcn/Tailwind restraint

visual_language.whitespace_usage: quiet whitespace for entry pages, dense organization for work surfaces
visual_language.visual_weight_distribution: light chrome; data, code, and lists carry most visual mass
visual_language.focal_strategy: entry pages focus on a single input/action; workspace pages focus on scan and selection
visual_language.contrast_level: medium-high for text, low for chrome
visual_language.texture_usage: semantic stripes/hatches only when representing diff states

composition.hierarchy_method: spatial isolation, type weight, functional pane layout, and muted metadata
composition.balance_type: centered on entry pages, asymmetric workspace on data pages
composition.flow_direction: entry pages top-to-bottom; workspaces left-to-right from context to detail
composition.grouping_strategy: toolbars, sidebars, lists, and content panes grouped by borders and spacing
composition.negative_space_role: calms entry pages and keeps dense work surfaces readable

imagery.graphic_elements: logo, file/status icons, red/green semantic markers, line numbers, compact metadata
imagery.image_shape: not image-led; UI surfaces are rectangular with small radii

interaction_feel.hover_behavior: muted controls gain subtle background or foreground contrast; no decorative motion
interaction_feel.loading_style: functional status rows or skeleton rows, not decorative
interaction_feel.microinteraction_density: moderate in toolbars/sidebars, low on entry pages

brand_voice_in_ui.tone: direct developer-facing with a small informal edge
brand_voice_in_ui.cta_style: utility-first action rather than marketing CTA

overview.fallback_strategy: reduce to static CSS surfaces and instant state changes
overview.primary_technology: CSS, SVG icons, virtualized React surfaces where needed

particle_systems.description: not part of the repo visual language

3d_elements.description: not part of the repo visual language

shader_effects.description: not part of the repo visual language

scroll_morphing.description: not present; use contained scrolling and sticky chrome instead

text_effects.description: text can be enhanced by code/status colors and compact metadata, not animated typography

cursor_effects.description: not part of the repo visual language

canvas_drawings.description: not part of the repo visual language

svg_animations.description: SVG icons may be present but should not be animated

visual_effects.composite_notes: Avoid decorative web effects. The repo-wide feel comes from precise layout, restrained chrome, compact data rows, sticky/contained work surfaces, and semantic red/green state markers.
